info.title: BOSS Location Management API
info.description:
Manage locations and disposal for the Back of Store System, including available
disposal, recycling, and donation options.

Unless otherwise noted, the following limits apply to fields in URL path
and query params:
- Date/time strings must be provided in ISO 8601 format. All values converted to
  UTC, no time zone info will be retained.
- All string fields, path and query parameters are limited to 127 characters or
  fewer
- All UUIDs are UUIDv4. These will be generated initially by SmarterX, and used in
  subsequent requests to identify options.

# General Features

Endpoints that return a list of resources will include pagination info in the
response body, and paging may be controlled with query parameters. These endpoints
will also typically include query parameters to filter the list of items by name,
description, location or other fields.

For items that support DELETE operations, these are considered "soft deletes" which
will prevent the item from being returned in GET requests, but will not remove it
from the database. The items may be restored by performing a PATCH with the body
`{"discarded": false}`.

# Location Management

The following use cases are supported:
- Creating new locations
- Updating existing locations
- Removing locations
- Retrieving one or more locations, with optional parameters to filter by name,
  description or location
- Enabling or disabling single or multiple disposal options at a location

*Note*: API users are responsible for enforcing validity of options for a 
location. For example, assigning a State Toxic disposal option for a 
location in a state that does not have state-level regulations may result
in undesirable recommendation results.

# Option Management

The following use cases are supported:
- Updating existing option names and descriptions
- Retrieving one or more options, with optional parameters to filter by name,
  description, or category
- Enabling or disabling an option for multiple locations
- Removing options (soft delete)

With the exception of Donation Partners, new options must be created by 
SmarterX, since they often require new rule sets and other configuration
behind the scenes to ensure proper bin results. Our future roadmap includes
self-service rule writing for retail customers via Smarter-1, but this is
out of scope for v1 of Location Management.

# Donation Option Management

The following use cases are supported:
- Creating new donation options
- Updating existing donation options (names, descriptions, tax IDs, POC info, etc.)
- Retrieving one or more donation options, with optional parameters to filter by name,
  description, or location
- Removing donation options (soft delete)

Donation options contain additional fields for linking to MealConnect, and for
storing contact info and other details about the donation partner.

get.description: Get a details for a single location

delete.description:
Soft-delete a location. This will prevent the location from being returned
in GET requests, but will not remove it from the database. The location may
be restored by performing a PATCH with the body `{"discarded": false}`.

get.summary: Get enabled options for a location
get.description:
Gets all binning options enabled available at a particular location.
Returns 404 if the location isn't found.

put.summary: Enable only specified options at a location
put.description:
Enable multiple options at a location. Any options not included in the request body
will be disabled. If the response is not a 204 (e.g. 400, 404), no options will be
modified.

patch.summary: Enable several options at a location
patch.description:
Enable options at a location. Any options not included will not be modified.
If the response is not a 204 (e.g. 400, 404), no options will be modified.

patch.summary: Disable several options at a location
patch.description:
Disable several options at a location. Any options not included will not be modified.
If the response is not a 204 (e.g. 400, 404), no options will be modified.

get.summary: Get whether an option is enabled at a location
get.description:
Gets whether an option is enabled at a particular location. Returns 404 if
the location or option isn't found.

put.summary: Enable an option at a location

delete.summary: Disable an option at a location

get.description:
Gets all binning options available to a customer. This includes options
that are not enabled at any location.
Get a details for a single option

Gets all locations where a particular option is enabled. Returns 404 if the
option isn't found.

put.description:
Enable an option at multiple locations. Any locations not included
in the request body will have the option disabled. If the response 
is not a 204 (e.g. 400, 404), no locations will be modified.
put.summary: Enable an option only at specified locations

patch.description:
Enable an option at multiple locations. Any locations not included
will not be modified. If the response is not a 204 (e.g. 400, 404),
no locations will be modified.
patch.summary: Enable an option at multiple locations

patch.description:
Disable an option at multiple locations. Any locations not included
will not be modified. If the response is not a 204 (e.g. 400, 404),
no locations will be modified.
patch.summary: Disable an option at multiple locations

get.description:
Gets all donation options available to a customer. This includes options
that are not enabled at any location.
Get a details for a single donation option

put.description: Update a donation option (replaces option info)

delete.description:
Soft-delete a donation option. This will prevent the option from being returned
in GET requests, but will not remove it from the database. The option may
be restored by performing a PATCH with the body `{"discarded": false}`.

Pagination.description: Pagination info for a list of resources. Pages start at 1 (1-indexed).

Restore-Discarded.description:
Data to restore a discarded resource. This is used to perform a PATCH to
restore a discarded location or option.

Error-Messages.description:
A list of error messages. This is returned in the body of a 400 response
if the request is invalid, or a 404 response.

Timestamps.description:
Timestamps for a resource. These are generated by SmarterX, and will be
ignored if provided in a POST/PUT/PATCH requests.

Location-Update.description:
Data for a single location, e.g. a physical store. These are the fields
required to perform a PATCH to update an existing location.

zip.description:
Zip or postal code for the location. US zip codes must be a valid
5-digit code, e.g. "98039" and Canadian postal codes must be a 
valid 6-character e.g. "V6B 1A1".

country.description:
Country code for the location, in ISO 3166 alpha-2 format. Currently only US
and CA are supported. If not provided, defaults to "US"

mealconnect_id.description:
ID of the location in the MealConnect API. This is used to link this location
to the correct DonorLocation in MealConnect. New locations *must* be created *in
advance* by Feeding America. This field may be left null for locations that
don't participate in MealConnect.

Location.description: Data for a single location, e.g. a physical store

New-Location.description:
Data for a single location, e.g. a physical store. These are the fields
required to POST a new location, or PUT updated location info.

Option-Update.description:
Data to PATCH (partially update) a single sorting option. May provide
all or a subset of fields to update.

Option.description: Data for a single sorting option

Donation-Option-Base.description: Additional info added to donation options (`"category": "donation"`)

zip.description:
Zip or postal code for the location. US zip codes must be a valid
5-digit code, e.g. "98039" and Canadian postal codes must be a 
valid 6-character e.g. "V6B 1A1".

country.description:
Country code for the location, in ISO 3166 alpha-2 format. Currently only US
and CA are supported. If not provided, defaults to "US"

mealconnect.description:
Whether to link this option to MealConnect. Donations that use this option,
and are reported to the BOSS reporting API, will be reported to Feeding America
via the MealConnect API, and pickup will be coordinated with local food banks.

poc_name.description:
Name of the point of contact at the donation location. This is used to
coordinate pickups with local food banks.

poc_email.description:
Email of the point of contact at the donation location. This is used to
coordinate pickups with local food banks.

poc_phone.description:
Phone number of the point of contact at the donation location. This is used to
coordinate pickups with local food banks.

Donation-Option-Update.description:
Data to PATCH (partially update) a single donation option. May provide
all or a subset of fields for update.

Donation-Option.description:
Data for a single donation option. These are the fields returned by GET
requests for donation options.

New-Donation-Option.description:
Data for a single donation option. These are the fields required to create a
new donation option, or perform a PUT to update an existing donation option.

Location-Update.description:
Data for a single location, e.g. a physical store. These are the fields
required to perform a PATCH to update an existing location.

New-Location.description:
Data for a single location, e.g. a physical store. These are the fields
required to POST a new location, or PUT updated location info.

Option-Update.description:
Data to PATCH (partially update) a single sorting option. Only name/description
updates are accepted.

Donation-Option-Update.description:
Data to PATCH (partially update) a single donation option. May provide
all or a subset of fields for update.

New-Donation-Option.description:
Data for a single donation option. These are the fields required to create a
new donation option, or perform a PUT to update an existing donation option.

Location-Names.description:
List of location names, used when bulk-enabling or disabling an option for multiple
locations.

Option-Ids.description: List of option_ids, used when bulk-enabling or disabling options at a single location.

NotFound.description: Returned in the body of a 404 response if a customer, location or option doesn't exist. The message will explain which one wasn't found.

InvalidPost.description:
Returned in the body of a 400 response if the request is invalid. The
messages will explain what's wrong with the request.

IdListError.description:
If a request contains multiple location_names or option_ids, and there's a problem
one or more of the IDs, this will be returned in the body of a 400 response, along
with messages explaining what's wrong with the request. Errors might include:
  
  - Invalid types (e.g. a location_name that's not a string)
  - Invalid UUIDs (e.g. an option_id that's not a UUIDv4)
  - Unknown locations (e.g. a location_name that doesn't exist)
  - Unknown options (e.g. an option_id that doesn't exist)

Enabled.description: Whether an option is enabled at a location

location_name.name: location_name
location_name.description:
Customer provided, unique name for a location. This location name must be
previously registered with SmarterX.

option_id.name: option_id
option_id.description:
UUID of the option. This option must be previously registered with
SmarterX.

street_address.name: street_address
street_address.description: Search using street address. Wildcards (`*`) are supported.

city.name: city
city.description: Search using city. Wildcards (`*`) are supported.

state.name: state
state.description:
Search using state. Use commas or spaces to separate multiple states in
a list, the search will return locations in any of the listed states.

zip.name: zip
zip.description:
Search using zip. Use commas or spaces to separate multiple zip codes
in a list, the search will return locations in any of the listed zip
codes.

name.name: name
name.description: Search using name. Wildcards (`*`) are supported.

description.name: description
description.description: Search using description. Wildcards (`*`) are supported.

category.name: category
category.description: Search using category. Must use one of the allowed values.

order.name: order
order.description: Sort results by the specified field, in the given direction (`<field>_<direction>`).

options.name: options
options.description:
Whether to include options in the response. If false, only location info
will be returned.

discarded.name: discarded
discarded.description:
Whether to include discarded locations in the response. If false, only
non-discarded locations will be returned.
